new_criterion_template: python3 -c 'import json,sys,urllib.request as u; r=json.load(u.urlopen("http://localhost:8000/api/jobs/all")); j=next((x for x in r["jobs"] if x["id"]=="<JOB_ID>"), None); assert j is not None, "job missing"; assert j.get("status") != "manifest", f"status still manifest: {j}"; assert j.get("status") in ("running","ok","failed","not_loaded","unknown"), f"status not in known set: {j}"; print("OK", j["id"], j["status"])'
justification: launchctl print does not expose next-fire-time for any launchd trigger type (KeepAlive, StartInterval, StartCalendarInterval). Confirmed empirically by phase-23.5.14 (CONDITIONAL, violation_type Invalid_Precondition) AND by 5+ authoritative sources in phase-23.5.13.3 research brief (launchd.plist man page, launchctl man page, Apple developer docs, dabrahams gist, live launchctl print). Amendment replaces the unmeetable assertion with a status-set check validating that _classify_launchctl_state in cron_dashboard_api.py returned a documented value.

The structured_log + _read_audit_tail + _AUDIT_JSONL_PATH + _AUDIT_TAIL_LIMIT symbols are referenced by sovereign_api.py:461-465 AND test_observability.py:36-37 but do NOT exist in harness_autoresearch.py (verified via grep).
